title: Pediatric CDS Module Manifest
description: EP5-T2 (DEF-5) formalizes modules/<id>/module.json's shape, superseding the ad-hoc field-presence checks that used to live entirely inside scripts/validate-kb.mjs. `status` is SPIKE-006 RQ4's closed lifecycle enum (`unsigned-stub` -> `integrity-recorded` -> `superseded`/`revoked`); `clinicalContentHash`/`governanceHash` are the SPIKE-006 Amendment 1 two-part digest (`sha256:<64 hex>`), which this schema requires to be present and well-formed for every status EXCEPT `unsigned-stub` (a freshly scaffolded, not-yet-signed module legitimately has neither yet). `approvedBy` mirrors rule.schema.json's `clinicalApprovers` field exactly: `maxItems: 0` makes a populated approver list a hard schema violation, not merely a test failure (D-4) — no credentialed human clinician has approved this knowledge base, and ARC/council-review/rf output is explicitly not a valid source for one. `supersedes: null` and `approvedBy: []` are legitimately-empty values on a first release (AC-WP5-RESIL) and are valid under every status this schema accepts; by contrast this schema also requires `validationRunId` to be a non-empty string specifically when `status` is `integrity-recorded` (SPIKE-006 Amendment 1: a manifest may be integrity-recorded at the content layer without yet being servable, but never servable without one — src/kbVerify.js#verifyManifest is the runtime enforcement of that same must-not-be-empty rule). Structural validity here never implies clinical validity, safety, or that a named human clinician reviewed anything.

id.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Must match the module's directory name under modules/ — that cross-check is enforced by scripts/validate-kb.mjs (this schema cannot see the filesystem), not by this pattern alone.

title.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Human-readable module display name.

schemaVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Integer manifest-shape version, bumped only when this schema's own shape changes.

status.description: REQUIRED, non-null, closed enum (SPIKE-006 RQ4) — no other value, including the earlier draft's "signed", is legal. `unsigned-stub` = scaffolded, no digest yet. `integrity-recorded` = the two-part digest below matches recorded content and, per src/kbVerify.js#READY_STATUS, is the only status the server/build/browser will serve. `superseded`/`revoked` = a former integrity-recorded release that has been replaced or withdrawn; it keeps its digests for audit but is no longer servable.

knowledgeBaseVersion.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Cross-checked for agreement with other KB version markers by scripts/validate-kb.mjs, not by this schema.

evidenceReviewedThrough.description: REQUIRED, non-null. The staleness anchor named in SPIKE-006 Amendment 4 — the field an eventual expiry policy will measure against. This schema validates only that it is a well-formed date; the expiry window itself is an undetermined governance decision (Amendment 4) and is out of scope here.

engineLabel.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Human-readable label for the deterministic rule engine serving this module.

supportedAgeMonths.description: REQUIRED, non-null. Both keys REQUIRED. This schema validator has no cross-field comparison keyword, so `min < max` is not checked here — see scripts/validate-kb.mjs if that invariant needs enforcing.

clinicalContentHash.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable ONLY when `status` is `unsigned-stub` (enforced below by the `allOf`/`if`/`then`/`else` conditional, since a plain `type` union cannot express "null here, never there"). SPIKE-006 Amendment 1's SHA-256 digest over the four KB JSON files' parsed values plus raw-byte hashes of the two source files that still hardcode clinical thresholds. A hash match proves content-provenance consistency with a prior release — never clinical validity.

governanceHash.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable ONLY when `status` is `unsigned-stub` (same conditional as `clinicalContentHash`). SPIKE-006 Amendment 1's second SHA-256 digest, over this manifest's own governance fields (src/kbVerify.js#GOVERNANCE_FIELD_KEYS).

approvedBy.description: REQUIRED key, and under this schema version it MUST be exactly `[]` — mirrors schemas/rule.schema.json's `clinicalApprovers` field exactly. `maxItems: 0` makes a populated approver list a hard schema violation, not merely a test failure (D-4/AC-D4). `[]` means NO credentialed clinical approval yet; this is legitimately empty on every release this schema accepts, never a defect. No credentialed human clinician has approved this knowledge base, and ARC/council-review/rf output is explicitly NOT a valid source for one. Raising `maxItems` is the deliberate, reviewable act by which this project would first claim clinical sign-off; it must never be done to make a build pass.

validationRunId.description: REQUIRED key. Nullable in general (a manifest can be `integrity-recorded` at the content layer before a validation run id is attached), but this schema requires it to be a non-empty string specifically when `status` is `integrity-recorded` (enforced below) — matching src/kbVerify.js#verifyManifest's must-not-be-empty treatment of this field for servability (AC-WP5-RESIL). Never invented (SPIKE-006 Amendment 1): a null here is honest, not a validation gap.

supersedes.description: REQUIRED key, legitimately nullable (AC-WP5-RESIL) — `null` is the normal, valid value for a first release with no prior version, under every status this schema accepts. When non-null, points to the prior release's `knowledgeBaseVersion`.

releasedAt.description: REQUIRED key, legitimately nullable — `null` before a release timestamp exists. When non-null, an RFC 3339 date-time.

module_topic.description: OPTIONAL. Evidence Foundry P1-T2 (docs/project_plans/expansion/02-evidence-foundry-on-research-foundry.md §3.2) module-variable-envelope field. Not required here — modules/anemia/module.json predates this field and stays valid without it. Presence/content checks beyond basic type live in scripts/validate-kb.mjs (field-presence validation only, per this plan's OQ-7 ruling), not in this schema.

intended_hcp_users.description: OPTIONAL. §3.2 envelope field — see `module_topic`'s description for the presence-vs-schema split rationale.

patient_population.description: OPTIONAL. §3.2 envelope field (age_min_months/age_max_months_exclusive/settings) — see `module_topic`'s description. Sub-shape is intentionally not constrained here (field-presence validation only).

evidence_policy.description: OPTIONAL. §3.2 envelope field (recent_window_days/older_source_rule/exact_passage_required_for_implementable_claim) — see `module_topic`'s description. Sub-shape is intentionally not constrained here (field-presence validation only).
